manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to describe a target word for another player to guess.

Rules:
(a) You have to reply in the form: CLUE: <some text>. Guesses from the other player will start with GUESS.
(b) You cannot use the target word itself, parts or morphological variants of it in your description.
(c) In addition, the same rules apply for related words which are provided below.

End conditions:
(i) If you use the target word or a related word in your description, then you lose.
(ii) If the other player can guess the target word in 3 tries, you both win.

Let us start.

This is the target word that you need to describe and that the other player needs to guess:

alabama

Related words are:

- usa
- state
- river

Important: You are under time pressure, give short descriptions that are to the point!

message.content: CLUE: Home of the Crimson Tide college football team.
message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue, not use "alabama" or related words: usa, state, river. Can't use "USA", "state", "river", or any morphological variants. Also cannot use "Alabam", "Alabama". Need to give a clue that points to Alabama without those words. Avoid "south", "birmingham", "montgomery", "mobile", "Tuscaloosa", "college", "football", "cotton", "delta". "Gulf" maybe? "Gulf" ok? "Gulf" is not a related word. Could say: "Home of Crimson Tide football team". But "football" possibly okay. Could use "crimson tide". That is a phrase but may be okay. Not using related words. "Crimson Tide" is a nickname. Could reference "Huntsville rocket center". Also "the heart of the Deep South". Could use "Deep South" might be okay. Avoid "state". Others: "The birthplace of the civil rights movement". But might be too vague.

We must be within limit of short. Provide one clue. Ensure no restricted words.

We'll give: CLUE: Home of the Crimson Tide college football team. That mentions football but not "state" or "river" or "USA". Works.
